mechanism_narrative: A high absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to lower equity valuations. This effect is most pronounced when the policy rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, signaling a drag on future growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% is the highest since 2001
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, which translates directly into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth. This positive economic momentum supports equity market appreciation over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent positive growth over the past 3 years
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) following months with strong (top quartile) 3-month changes in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between weakening housing construction (HOUST) and still-strong retail sales (RSAFS) signals an impending economic slowdown. The negative wealth effect from the housing downturn will eventually curb consumer spending, leading investors to price in lower future earnings.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in HOUST < -10% while 6-month change in RSAFS > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has declined significantly from its 2006 peak
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, strong growth in industrial production (INDPRO) is interpreted negatively by markets. It signals potential for rising inflation, prompting expectations of further Fed rate hikes, which increases the discount rate and compresses equity multiples.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is in a rising trend over the prior 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been raising rates since 2004
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling to a cyclical low signals a late-cycle economy at risk of overheating. This condition leads to wage pressures, rising inflation, and pre-emptive monetary tightening by the central bank, which is bearish for equities over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% is near the lows of the previous cycle
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.7%, the average 5-day S&P 500 forward return is not statistically different from periods when UNRATE is above 4.7%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest indicator of economic expansion. This growth underpins corporate earnings expectations and investor sentiment, providing a persistent tailwind for equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP has grown consistently year-over-year since the 2001 recession
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 60-day forward returns to the year-over-year percentage change in GDPC1 is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) beyond a moderate threshold forces the Federal Reserve to pursue more restrictive monetary policy. The prospect of higher rates to combat inflation leads to negative revisions for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation has been volatile and periodically exceeded 3% in recent years
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative difference when YoY CPI is above 3.0% compared to when it is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) while the labor market remains tight (low UNRATE) is a classic leading indicator of recession. The construction sector's decline precedes broader job losses, and forward-looking equity markets price in this impending downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have fallen sharply from their peak while unemployment remains low
failure_condition: The median 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive during periods when the trigger condition is met.
